title: Adjust dependency constraints for Biblicus + RCA stack compatibility
description: Update Plexus and Tactus dependency constraints to enforce Biblicus max_exemplars support and prevent RCA stack resolver drift.

comments.author: derek
comments.text: Starting implementation: tightening source-level dependency constraints in Tactus and Plexus pyproject.toml to remove resolver drift and RCA breakage.

comments.author: derek
comments.text: Applied source-level dependency constraints: Plexus pins hdbscan=0.8.40 and umap-learn=0.5.7; Tactus now requires biblicus>=1.10.0,<2.0.0 (max_exemplars-compatible). Pending user review; no commit performed.

comments.author: derek
comments.text: Verification run complete. Tactus: lock+sync successful in isolated Poetry venv, resolved biblicus 1.10.4 with ReinforcementMemory.__init__ containing max_exemplars. Plexus: lock+sync successful in isolated .venv, but still resolves biblicus 1.9.1 because dependency is tactus 0.46.2 from package index. Result: source constraint updates alone in local Tactus are not consumed by Plexus unless Plexus points to local Tactus path or released Tactus version containing the new Biblicus constraint.

comments.author: derek
comments.text: Created branch chore/plx-9baee9-rca-dependency-constraints from develop to preserve current Plexus dependency work-in-progress while Tactus release PR is pending. Preparing checkpoint commit with pyproject/lock/env settings and Kanbus artifacts.
